column1: # SIPRI Yearbook Chapter 7 — World Nuclear Forces indicator catalog
# Source: SIPRI Yearbook 2024
#   PDF: https://www.sipri.org/sites/default/files/YB24%2007%20WNF.pdf
#   Version: YB24 07 WNF.pdf (97 pages
#   License: free academic with attribution; cite the Yearbook edition.
#   Attribution: see docs/source-attributions.md
# This file is the single source of truth for which SIPRI Yearbook Ch.7
# indicators are read in Stage 2. Every Stage 2 adapter call resolves its
# raw_column from this file. Adding/removing an indicator here is the only
# change needed to widen or narrow the SIPRI Yearbook Ch.7 ingestion.
# Architecture design: docs/architecture/sipri_yearbook_ch7.md §3.4
# --- MISSING-DATA / ANNOTATION CONVENTION ---
# The SIPRI Yearbook Table 7.1 uses three special tokens in numeric cells:
#   "–"  (U+2013 EN DASH) — nil or a negligible value (per Table 7.1 legend).
#       Treated as 0 in normalized_value; raw_value preserves the literal "–"
#       for the audit trail so a reviewer can distinguish "truly zero retired
#       warheads" from "unknown retired warheads".
#   ".." (two ASCII FULL STOP characters) — not applicable or not available
#       (per Table 7.1 legend). Treated as NULL in normalized_value;
#       raw_value preserves the literal ".." for the audit trail.
#   "c. <num> [letter]" (e.g.
#       an approximate warhead count with a footnote letter referring to
#       SIPRI's notes. The leading "c. " prefix and the footnote letter
#       suffix are stripped before parsing the integer; the parsed integer
#       becomes normalized_value. The raw_value preserves the full original
#       cell string (e.g.
# --- COUNTRY DENYLIST ---
# The Table 7.1 in the PDF contains one aggregate row ("Total") that is NOT
# a country. The Stage 2 adapter filters it via _SIPRI_YEARBOOK_CH7_NON_COUNTRY_LABELS
# (frozenset containing "Total" and "World"). Only the 9 nuclear-armed state
# rows reach the wide frame. The fixture exercises this with the "Total" row.
# --- 3-INDICATOR CHOICE ---
# Table 7.1 has 5 numeric columns: Deployed
# Total inventory. The catalog extracts 3 of these:
#   total_inventory — Total inventory column. The headline count of all
#       warheads (deployed + stored + retired awaiting dismantlement).
#   deployed — Military stockpile / Deployed column. The count of warheads
#       in operational deployment (on missiles or at operational bases).
#   retired — Retired warheads column. The count of warheads awaiting
#       dismantlement (the disarmament pipeline). higher_is_better=False
#       because more retired = more disarmament activity = better peace
#       signal (per the score module's inversion convention).
# The Stored and Stockpile total columns are derived (Stored = Stockpile
# total - Deployed; Stockpile total = Deployed + Stored) and are not
# extracted as independent indicators. They can be recomputed by the
# score module if needed.
variable_name
sipri_yearbook_ch7_nuclear_warheads_total_inventory
column8: Total inventory of nuclear warheads (deployed + stored + retired awaiting dismantlement). The headline SIPRI Yearbook Ch.7 figure; captures the absolute size of a state's nuclear arsenal. Higher = bigger arsenal = more nuclear capability.

column1: sipri_yearbook_ch7_nuclear_warheads_deployed
column8: Number of nuclear warheads in operational deployment (on missiles or at operational bases). The "ready to use" count; captures operational nuclear readiness. Higher = more operationally alert warheads.

column1: sipri_yearbook_ch7_nuclear_warheads_retired
column8: Number of nuclear warheads awaiting dismantlement (the disarmament pipeline). Higher = more warheads in retirement = more disarmament activity (inverted: higher retired = better peace score per the Stage 5 score module convention).
